prompt: I'm building a new Go CLI tool — single Go module, ships as a cross-platform binary. Set me up with mise: build, test, lint, and fmt for day-to-day work, plus a release flow using goreleaser. It's just one Go module so I'd rather have flat task names than a `go:` prefix on everything. Docs get built with mkdocs if that matters.
assertions: FLAT task names honored: build/test/lint/fmt at top level (no go: prefix)
every task file follows the contract
a goreleaser release flow was generated (asked-for niche task)
command-skill for the destructive release/publish step
no aws-auth helper (no AWS in this project)
